# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.
# Category: sql_injection

function generateReport($pdo) {
    $filters = [];
    $query = "SELECT * FROM reports WHERE 1=1";
    
    if (!empty($_POST['status'])) {
        $status = $_POST['status'];
        $filters[] = "status = :status";
    }

    if (!empty($_POST['category'])) {
        $category = $_POST['category'];
        $filters[] = "category = :category";
    }

    if (!empty($_POST['start_date']) && !empty($_POST['end_date'])) {
        $start_date = $_POST['start_date'];
        $end_date = $_POST['end_date'];
        $filters[] = "date >= :start_date AND date <= :end_date";
    }

    if (count($filters) > 0) {
        $query .= " AND " . implode(" AND ", $filters);
    }

    $stmt = $pdo->prepare($query);

    if (!empty($_POST['status'])) {
        $stmt->bindValue(':status', $status, PDO::PARAM_STR);
    }

    if (!empty($_POST['category'])) {
        $stmt->bindValue(':category', $category, PDO::PARAM_STR);
    }

    if (!empty($_POST['start_date'])) {
        $stmt->bindValue(':start_date', $start_date, PDO::PARAM_STR);
    }

    if (!empty($_POST['end_date'])) {
        $stmt->bindValue(':end_date', $end_date, PDO::PARAM_STR);
    }

    $stmt->execute();
    return $stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
}
